title: pelta palustris
intro: Pelta palustris, also known as Marsh Horsetail, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Equisetaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as caffeic acid and ferulic acid. The medicinal properties of Pelta palustris have been reported to offer various health benefits, including antioxidant, antifungal, and anti-diabetic activities. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosage.
